Increased SPX/VIX beta ahead of VIX expo - Intraday post (16/Sept)

Monthly post:

SEPTEMBER - Monthly & Weekly post (02-05/Sept)

Weekly post:

OpEx week, FOMC, Risk of Reversion - Weekly post (15-19/Sept)

Also dropped a new post on Twitter about probabilities and right-tail. More in details in the educational post section. We have VIX expo today with longer SPX/VIX beta:

Let’s look at the skewed Galton-board of today…

INTRADAY POSTCoding today’s positioning:

We have a heavily sold left-tail today and a locally sold right-tail as well, drawning a long fly pattern, with net a net heavily negative speed profile, so the distribution is heavily right-skewed and vol of vol is being sold. The center of this fly patter lies at 6625, and market expects the most supportive (“vol swallowing”) liquiditiy at 6590.

The centroid of the profile is in the 6594.66 - 6607.65 area. This is a very supportive area especially during the PM.

Upside target is 6631.09, and this is the upside pivot as well. It breaks only if put skew being sold heavily and this vol suppression appears in the realized volatility, bcs there is a vanna flip here as well. Then, it can go as high as 6645 and pin to 6635 during the power hour.But the market’s main bet is suppressed volatility around 6600.

The centroid can be broken only during the AM.If it does, 6565 will pin, no lower downside target. As said in the weekly post, market expects VIX to get pinned back to the downside. With heavy short vanna structure and increased spot/vol correlation, this should cap the momentum and pin it around the most liquidity area, and as said this is the market’s main bet. But due to the decreased negative spot/vol beta, the VIX expo’s effect on spot will be lowered, as there is less beta hedge required.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
